SEARCH
Package basicFiles.files

Class BinaryFile

java.lang.Object
basicFiles.files.BaseFile
basicFiles.files.BinaryFile
public class BinaryFile extends BaseFile
Class representing a binary file. Allows writing and reading a serializable Java object, without enforcing a specific extension (e.g., .bin).
Author:
Elias Kassas

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    BinaryFile(File folder, String fileName, String extension)
    Constructor for a binary file with a configurable extension.
    BinaryFile(String fileName, String extension)
    Constructor for a binary file without a folder.

  • Constructor Details

    • BinaryFile

      public BinaryFile(File folder, String fileName, String extension) throws FileNotFoundException, ClassNotFoundException, IOException
      Constructor for a binary file with a configurable extension.
      Parameters:
      folder - Folder containing the file.
      fileName - File name without extension.
      extension - File extension (e.g.: ".dat", ".save", ".bkp").
      Throws:
      FileNotFoundException
      ClassNotFoundException
      IOException

    • BinaryFile

      public BinaryFile(String fileName, String extension) throws FileNotFoundException, ClassNotFoundException, IOException
      Constructor for a binary file without a folder.
      Parameters:
      fileName - File name without extension.
      extension - File extension.
      Throws:
      FileNotFoundException
      ClassNotFoundException
      IOException

  • Method Details

    • write

      public void write(Object... args) throws IOException
      Description copied from class: BaseFile
      Writes data to the file, overwriting existing content.
      Specified by:
      write in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Variable arguments used by subclasses for specialized writing.
      Throws:
      IOException

    • read

      public void read() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Description copied from class: BaseFile
      Reads the file content from the disk. Specific formatting and processing are handled by concrete subclasses.
      Specified by:
      read in class BaseFile
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during disk access.
      ClassNotFoundException - If an object read requires a missing class.
      FileNotFoundException

    • getContent

      public Object getContent()
      Returns the object loaded from the file.
      Returns:
      The object loaded, or null if empty.

    • clear

      public void clear() throws IOException
      Clears the content of the file and memory.
      Throws:
      IOException

    • deletePhysicalFile

      public boolean deletePhysicalFile()
      Physically removes the file from the file system.
      Specified by:
      deletePhysicalFile in class BaseFile
      Returns:
      true if deletion succeeded, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      FileNotFoundException

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class BaseFile

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the file's internal data buffer is currently empty. This method evaluates the presence of loaded data rather than checking the physical file size on disk.
      Returns:
      true if the file content has not been initialized or is explicitly null; false otherwise.

    • append

      public void append(Object... args) throws Exception
      Description copied from class: BaseFile
      Appends data to the end of the file.
      Specified by:
      append in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Variable arguments used by subclasses for specialized appending.
      Throws:
      Exception - If an error occurs during the append operation.
